title: Nectandra aurantiodora
taxonomy: Nectandra aurantiodora is classified within the family Laurus, which is part of the order Laurales. This species falls under the genus Nectandra, which consists of evergreen trees native to the Americas. The classification of Nectandra aurantiodora further breaks down into the kingdom Plantae, with the division Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida. The order Laurales, to which it belongs, contains other families such as the Siparunaceae. Overall, this classification provides a comprehensive understanding of the species' position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Nectandra aurantiodora is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae, commonly known as a tree or shrub native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Nectandra aurantiodora include sesquiterpenes, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
